coverage.evidence: Digits and CIFAR grid metrics plus merge landscape figures.
results/digits_merge/figures/per_task_basin_overlay.png.
Digits, CIFAR, and Qwen path/lambda sweeps.
Digits method table and overlay cover average, task arithmetic, SLERP, TIES, DARE, TIES+DARE, Fisher, RegMean, layer-wise task arithmetic, and validation grid search.
Digits, CIFAR, and pairwise single-digit conflict tables/figures.
Ten single-digit experts and all 45 pairwise merges.
Independent-initialization MLP path before/after Hungarian hidden-unit alignment.
CIFAR-10 vehicle/animal GroupNorm CNN merge landscape.
CIFAR100 ViT-style from-scratch transformer and ImageNet-pretrained ViT-B/16 frozen-backbone transfer merge studies are present.
Safetensors probe and same-file smoke test.
Qwen2.5-1.5B base-to-instruct path is evaluated with fixed NLL prompts plus GSM8K, MMLU, and HumanEval benchmark slices.
Qwen2.5-0.5B base, Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct, and Qwen2.5-Coder-0.5B-Instruct are evaluated in a two-expert merge plane.
results/fp_curvature_law/report.md compares diagonal-Fisher second-order midpoint predictions against real Qwen instruct/coder interpolation loss.
results/fp_merge_compare_dense/report.md evaluates a finite family containing linear average, task arithmetic, sign-elect, and magnitude-weighted variants, then selects by held-out worst-task NLL.
results/fp_gen_eval_dense/report.md evaluates base, endpoints, linear average, and unified lambda=0 on built-in math/code-output generation tasks without executing model-generated code.
Representative Qwen2.5-1.5B benchmark slices cover MMLU, GSM8K, HumanEval canonical-solution NLL, and BeaverTails safety/refusal NLL.

scripts/run_vllm_downstream_eval.py can build a served-model eval plan from the Qwen target registry; the generic registry run remains endpoint_unavailable, while checkpoint-specific hosted eval is tracked separately.

results/vllm_checkpoint_eval/qwen_0_5b_instruct_coder_uniform_average/report.md contains a real vLLM-hosted GSM8K/MMLU/safety/HumanEval compile eval for the materialized Qwen2.5-0.5B uniform-average checkpoint.
results/vllm_source_merge_comparison/report.md compares Qwen2.5-0.5B base/instruct/coder source endpoints against the materialized uniform-average checkpoint under the same vLLM downstream tasks.

results/probe_guided_dense_average_candidate/report.md selects a non-uniform Qwen instruct/coder bridge from the NLL grid, materializes the same-shape checkpoint locally, and records its real vLLM downstream eval.
coverage.item: Probe-guided dense average candidate vLLM eval

coverage.evidence: results/qwen_dense_module_guarded_candidate/report.md, results/qwen_dense_norm_guarded_candidate/report.md, and results/qwen_dense_selective_norm_guarded_candidate/report.md compare module-level, norm-only, and selective-norm tensor-rule variants against the global bridge under the same vLLM downstream tasks.
coverage.item: Qwen dense module-wise guard ablation vLLM eval

coverage.evidence: results/qwen_dense_sparse_method_candidate/report.md and results/qwen_dense_attention_sparse_method_candidate/report.md compare broad attention+MLP sparse rules against an attention-only sparse rule under real vLLM eval.
results/vllm_downstream_eval_smoke/smoke_report.md validates the OpenAI-compatible HTTP request, answer parsing, scoring, model ranking, and artifact writing path using a local mock endpoint.

results/vllm_checkpoint_eval_plan/report.md turns same-shape checkpoint candidates into one-checkpoint-at-a-time vLLM serve/eval commands while keeping missing checkpoints separate from completed metrics.
results/checkpoint_materialization_readiness/report.md audits writer commands, placeholders, dry-run outputs, checkpoint existence, and vLLM eval readiness in one table.

results/moe_materialization_pipeline_plan/report.md connects Qwen MoE target selection, topology, routing probe, readiness, route weights, expert remap, router-bias deltas, checkpoint writer, and vLLM eval gates.
results/probe_gated_unified_average_plan/report.md turns Dense vLLM ablations and toy MoE mechanism contrasts into a same-shape intervention gate rather than a static method ranking.
results/average_decision_report/report.md converts merge grids, conflict probes, and optional MoE routing probes into same-shape average decisions.
results/model_averaging_literature_review/report.md maps recent model averaging and MoE merging papers to probes, failure signals, and same-shape writer actions.
results/qwen_target_model_registry/report.md maps representative official, third-party, downstream, and adapter-pool Qwen candidates to scenarios, eval slices, probes, and same-shape topology gates.
results/moe_average_plan/report.md maps router/expert probes into same-shape router, shared-module, expert, and adapter averaging actions.
scripts/write_same_shape_average_checkpoint.py writes same-shape safetensors checkpoints; results/same_shape_writer_smoke/report.md validates Qwen2.5-0.5B base/instruct/coder dry-run compatibility.
results/dense_sparse_method_writer_smoke/report.md verifies coordinate-wise TIES-style trim/sign-elect/merge inside the same-shape checkpoint writer.

results/moe_tensor_rule_writer_smoke/report.md writes a tiny MoE-like safetensors checkpoint and verifies tensor-rule, freeze-router, router-bias additive deltas, and non-floating tensor behavior numerically.
results/moe_combined_writer_smoke/report.md verifies expert tensor rules, source expert alias remap, freeze-router, and router-bias additive deltas in one same-shape writer call.
results/moe_packed_expert_writer_smoke/report.md verifies first-dimension packed expert slice weights and source-expert remaps for Qwen-style packed MoE tensors.
results/moe_layerwise_expert_remap_smoke/report.md verifies layer-scoped source tensor alias rules for real multi-layer MoE expert matching.
results/checkpoint_topology_inspect/report.md inspects Qwen MoE/Dense configs and safetensors headers without loading weights.
results/average_candidate_recipes/report.md converts probe decisions into conservative same-shape materialization recipes and skips endpoint-only pseudo-averages.

results/moe_route_weight_recipes/report.md converts MoE routing/expert-load probes into tensor-rule files for same-shape checkpoint materialization; current recipe is waiting for real routing probe data.
results/moe_packed_route_weight_recipe_smoke/report.md verifies route/expert weights can emit Qwen-style packed_expert_rules.csv with source-expert remap columns.
results/moe_router_bias_plan/report.md converts expert_load.csv into writer-ready router-bias additive deltas for same-shape capacity correction.
results/moe_confidence_blended_router_bias_plan/report.md applies the same writer-ready capacity correction to the confidence-blended unified MoE candidate.
results/toy_moe_expert_weight_recipes/report.md converts calibration-searched per-expert source weights into same-shape checkpoint writer tensor rules.
results/toy_moe_output_projection_recipes/report.md converts route-conditioned output-space expert weights into same-shape checkpoint writer tensor rules.
results/toy_moe_confidence_blended_recipes/report.md converts projection-confidence-gated expert weights into same-shape checkpoint writer tensor rules.
results/moe_confidence_blended_combined_recipe/report.md composes expert weights, expert alias remap, and router-bias capacity deltas into one same-shape writer command.
results/moe_routing_readiness/report.md turns router_summary, route_overlap, and expert_load CSVs into router collapse, drift, boundary-fragility, and expert-load risk actions.

scripts/probe_moe_routing.py captures MoE router hooks and writes router_summary.csv, expert_load.csv, optional route_overlap.csv, summary.json, and report.md; results/moe_routing_probe_smoke/report.md validates the contract on a tiny local MoE.
results/moe_routing_probe_smoke/report.md proves the routing probe captures two tiny MoE gates and produces router, expert-load, token-route, comparison, and route-overlap CSVs.
results/toy_moe_merge/report.md runs a small same-shape MoE averaging experiment showing expert-index mismatch and expert-matched/router-calibrated fixes.
results/fp_moe_mechanism/report.md isolates function-preserving expert permutation, expert alignment, router calibration, and Fisher ablations with real forward/backward passes.
results/fp_moe_real_probe/report.md runs a function-preserving expert/router permutation on a real packed OLMoE checkpoint and shows same-name averaging fails unless expert identity is recovered.
results/moe_probe_gated_selector/report.md combines real OLMoE gauge evidence, Qwen3 expert correspondence, and toy route/capacity selection into a same-shape MoE average gate.

results/moe_unified_preflight_qwen3_30b/report.md verifies Qwen3-30B Instruct/Coder same-shape config, router tensor contract, routed expert layout, expert identity gate, and the emitted real routing probe command.

results/moe_routing_readiness/qwen3_30b_instruct_vs_coder/report.md analyzes the real Qwen3-30B Instruct/Coder route overlap and expert load probe, showing direct router averaging is high risk and needs calibration or freeze.
results/qwen3_moe_unified_route_guarded_candidate/report.md converts the real Qwen3 route/load probe into source-route-conditioned same-shape tensor rules and a validated writer dry-run command.
results/toy_moe_routing_readiness/report.md applies the generic readiness gate to toy MoE methods and flags all-weight routing drift separately from expert-matched/route-aware variants.
results/toy_moe_method_selection/report.md combines method metrics, routing readiness, and sparse capacity overflow into materialization gates plus a hard-top2/overflow Pareto frontier.
results/toy_moe_expert_remap_plan/report.md turns expert-output matching into source tensor alias rules for same-shape checkpoint materialization.
Dashboard includes a draggable precomputed merge-plane explorer with task-pair, method, objective, raw/normalized plane, alpha/beta, and lambda controls.

summary.interpretation: The exact-answer generation smoke follows the same boundary as the NLL selector: it is a fast behavioral check, not proof that a merge beats all endpoints. The unified candidate beats the linear midpoint on this smoke slice.

interpretation.finite_candidate_note: inside the candidate set, validation risk selection targets the empirical best candidate; residual risk is validation noise, not a static algorithm assumption
interpretation.selection_rule: choose the candidate with lowest held-out worst-task NLL, then report it on a disjoint test split
interpretation.unified_family: base + lambda * coordinate_rule(delta_instruct, delta_coder), with named methods as special cases
interpretation.why_this_is_unified: the method selector is a probe over mechanisms: barrier/coefficient, sign disagreement, magnitude importance, and optionally router policy

expert_identity_alignment.interpretation: same tensor name is not a stable expert identity after a legal MoE gauge permutation

mechanism_rows.interpretation: same tensor name is not a stable expert identity after a legal MoE gauge permutation
after experts move, the top-k router boundary must be re-fit to the merged experts
curvature weighting is not automatically safe; it must pass a held-out gate
router calibration can repair some dispatch drift even when expert weighting is imperfect
router-only fitting is limited if the averaged experts themselves mix unrelated functions

route_conditioned_fisher.interpretation: curvature weighting is not automatically safe; it must pass a held-out gate

router_calibration_after_alignment.interpretation: after experts move, the top-k router boundary must be re-fit to the merged experts

router_cannot_fix_misaligned_experts.interpretation: router-only fitting is limited if the averaged experts themselves mix unrelated functions

mechanism_deltas.interpretation: same tensor name is not a stable expert identity after a legal MoE gauge permutation
after experts move, the top-k router boundary must be re-fit to the merged experts
curvature weighting is not automatically safe; it must pass a held-out gate
router calibration can repair some dispatch drift even when expert weighting is imperfect
router-only fitting is limited if the averaged experts themselves mix unrelated functions

model_averaging_literature_review.dense_recommendation: Treat uniform averaging as a low-barrier baseline; escalate to conflict-aware, importance-aware, or coefficient-learned averages only when probes justify them.
model_averaging_literature_review.moe_recommendation: Do not merge router and experts blindly; run topology, routing, expert matching, and route-weight probes before materializing same-shape checkpoints.

summary.dense_recommendation: Treat uniform averaging as a low-barrier baseline; escalate to conflict-aware, importance-aware, or coefficient-learned averages only when probes justify them.
summary.moe_recommendation: Do not merge router and experts blindly; run topology, routing, expert matching, and route-weight probes before materializing same-shape checkpoints.

case_rows.evidence: same-name NLL degradation=5.491; aligned degradation=0.000000; permutation recovery=16/16
case_rows.same_shape_action: match expert identity first; apply any expert remap as source-slice alias, not as a shape change

case_rows.evidence: identity layers=1.000; argmax identity=1.000; diag/offdiag cosine ratio=1339.5
case_rows.same_shape_action: use identity expert slices first; freeze or small-step router until route overlap/load is measured

case_rows.probe: config and safetensors-header router/expert same-shape contract
case_rows.same_shape_action: run emitted route/load probe command; do not materialize until route readiness and behavior eval pass

case_rows.probe: real prompt route overlap + expert load readiness
case_rows.same_shape_action: keep identity expert layout, derive route/category-aware expert weights, freeze or calibrate router, then run vLLM behavior eval

case_rows.probe: task loss + route readiness + dispatch + capacity
case_rows.same_shape_action: select expert weights and router policy by serving mode; never expand expert count

stage_rows.required_evidence: tensor names, tensor shapes, router rows, expert dimension, tokenizer/model class

stage_rows.action_if_fail: apply layerwise expert alias/remap before any expert averaging
stage_rows.action_if_pass: use identity mapping for Qwen3 pair
stage_rows.gate: expert correspondence or gauge self-merge evidence
stage_rows.required_evidence: identity-optimal fraction or layerwise Hungarian permutation

stage_rows.action_if_fail: freeze expert source or stay at endpoint/base
stage_rows.action_if_pass: confidence-blend searched and output-projection expert weights

expert_identity_gate.reason: Expert identity is stable enough to use identity slices first.

unified_mechanism.current_qwen3_action: identity expert slices are allowed by correspondence; router and load remain blocked until real route probe.
unified_mechanism.moe_error_decomposition: expert gauge/identity + expert functional residual + router route-overlap drift + sparse capacity overflow + downstream behavior
unified_mechanism.objective: same-shape model average that can choose endpoint/base, layer/tensor coefficients, expert remap, guarded router, and capacity bias without changing architecture

dense_contrasts.interpretation: Uniform Dense averaging is not a safe default when task skills are not in the same low-loss basin.
dense_contrasts.mechanism: midpoint ridge and endpoint skill mismatch

dense_contrasts.interpretation: Global coefficient search transfers from NLL probe to a small vLLM gain, but still does not beat the best source.
dense_contrasts.mechanism: validation NLL selects a coder-anchored bridge away from the midpoint ridge

dense_contrasts.interpretation: A high conflict score identifies a mechanism, but the intervention must be narrower than a whole-module rule.
dense_contrasts.mechanism: module-level conflict is too coarse; freezing anchors and damping MLP removes useful adaptation

dense_contrasts.interpretation: Norm freezing is neutral on aggregate here, so it should remain an ablation knob rather than a default.
dense_contrasts.mechanism: normalization deltas shift task distribution rather than uniformly improving all tasks

dense_contrasts.interpretation: Freezing only extreme sign-conflict norm tensors hurts the real endpoint score.
dense_contrasts.mechanism: highest-conflict norm tensors are not sufficient causal levers

intervention_plan.evidence: bridge avg primary delta vs uniform = 0.023
intervention_plan.why: NLL barrier probe predicted the midpoint ridge and the vLLM eval confirmed a positive average-score delta.

intervention_plan.same_shape_action: do not freeze full embedding/norm groups or damp all MLP tensors by default
intervention_plan.why: The broad module action removed useful adaptation even though the conflict probe was real.

intervention_plan.same_shape_action: only use static tensor freezes inside a scored ablation sweep
intervention_plan.why: The targeted high-conflict norm freeze did not improve held-out endpoint behavior.

intervention_plan.same_shape_action: generate layer-scoped source_tensor_aliases before expert averaging
intervention_plan.trigger: expert output cosine or route behavior shows index mismatch
intervention_plan.why: MoE experts are not exchangeable by tensor name; matching recovers the largest failure component.

intervention_plan.same_shape_action: blend search weights and output-projection weights per expert using captured_fraction
intervention_plan.why: Projection is highly reliable for some experts and unreliable for others, so a single expert-weight rule is too coarse.

intervention_plan.evidence: matched router calibration soft worst-acc gain = 0.055
intervention_plan.same_shape_action: freeze, KD-calibrate, or small-lambda calibrate router only after route overlap checks
intervention_plan.trigger: expert weights changed or route overlap is below guard threshold
intervention_plan.why: Router prior and expert weights must be calibrated together; blind router averaging is not a valid default.

intervention_plan.same_shape_action: write router-bias deltas only when overflow reduction is worth the accuracy cost
intervention_plan.trigger: top-k load overflow exceeds capacity budget
intervention_plan.why: Capacity overflow is separate from NLL/accuracy; optimizing only accuracy leaves dispatch infeasible under sparse serving.

intervention_plan.same_shape_action: do not emit final Qwen MoE writer command until exact headers and real routing probe exist
intervention_plan.trigger: all probe-gated MoE components need real Qwen topology and routing inputs
intervention_plan.why: Toy/template writer readiness is not evidence that the real Qwen MoE tensors and routes are ready.

moe_contrasts.interpretation: Most of the toy MoE failure is recovered by matching expert output behavior before averaging.
moe_contrasts.mechanism: same-name expert tensors are semantically permuted

moe_contrasts.interpretation: A frozen router is safer than naive router averaging, but held-out router calibration adds measurable soft accuracy.
moe_contrasts.mechanism: expert weights and router dispatch must be co-calibrated

moe_contrasts.interpretation: Output-space projection gives the best soft-router score, but it is less robust under hard sparse dispatch.
moe_contrasts.mechanism: expert source weights should explain routed output residuals, not only parameter deltas

moe_contrasts.interpretation: Confidence blending trades a small soft loss for better hard top-2 sparse accuracy.
moe_contrasts.mechanism: projection is reliable for some experts and over-moves others

moe_contrasts.interpretation: Bias-only capacity correction sharply reduces overflow but costs some accuracy; keep it as a capacity gate.
moe_contrasts.mechanism: top-k capacity overflow is a separate failure mode from task loss

all_weight_average.description: average same-name router and expert tensors without expert matching

best_method.description: solve per-expert output-space source delta weights, then calibrate only the router

confidence_blended_expert_average.description: blend searched expert weights and output-space projection weights using projection captured fraction as confidence

confidence_blended_router_calibrated_ave.description: calibrate router after confidence-blended expert weights

expert_matched_average.description: align code experts to general source by output cosine before averaging

expert_matched_dare_average.description: align code experts and apply DARE to expert task vectors with the base router fixed

expert_matched_regmean_average.description: align code experts and RegMean-merge only expert linear layers with the base router fixed

expert_matched_ties_average.description: align code experts and apply TIES to expert task vectors with the base router fixed

expert_matched_ties_dare_average.description: align code experts and apply DARE+TIES to expert task vectors with the base router fixed

expert_output_projection_average.description: solve per-expert source delta weights from route-conditioned output-space residuals with the base router fixed

expert_output_projection_router_calibrat.description: solve per-expert output-space source delta weights, then calibrate only the router

expert_weight_search_average.description: search per-expert source delta weights on the calibration set with the base router fixed

expert_weight_search_router_calibrated_a.description: search per-expert source delta weights, then calibrate only the router

matched_router_calibrated_average.description: align code experts, freeze non-router tensors, and calibrate only the router

matched_router_frozen_average.description: align code experts by output cosine and keep the base router fixed

matched_router_hessian_average.description: align code experts and solve a Hessian-aware router average from source router distributions

matched_router_kd_average.description: align code experts and distill only the router from source model logits on unlabeled calibration inputs

matched_router_route_kd_average.description: align code experts and distill only the router from source route distributions on unlabeled calibration inputs

matched_router_sweep_selected_average.description: align code experts, then select router calibration KL by calibration worst loss

matched_router_topk_calibrated_average.description: align code experts and calibrate the router with hard top-k dispatch loss

matched_router_weight_search_average.description: align code experts and search router source-delta weights with route guards

route_aware_expert_average.description: freeze base router and use route-frequency expert source weights

unified_output_projection_moe_average.description: solve expert weights by output-space projection, then learn the same capacity-aware unified router objective

summary.command: python scripts/run_vllm_downstream_eval.py --base-url MOCK_BASE_URL --models mock-good,mock-bad --tasks gsm8k,mmlu,safety,humaneval_compile --example-source builtin --max-examples 2 --output-dir results/vllm_downstream_eval_smoke --timeout 10

mechanism_findings: Uniform averaging is dominated by all three source endpoints on avg primary score in this slice.
The base model wins the aggregate mostly because HumanEval compile is a syntax/loadability metric here, not because it is instruction-following aligned.
The uniform merge loses MMLU and GSM8K relative to the best endpoint and collapses HumanEval compile to zero, matching the earlier NLL ridge probe.
Safety policy accuracy of 0.500 is not a benign middle point: the merge keeps safe non-refusal at 1.0 but unsafe refusal at 0.0, so it almost never refuses unsafe prompts.
summary.next_method_hypothesis: Use source/task probes to learn non-uniform coefficients and module groups; do not average instruction/coder deltas uniformly when endpoint skills and safety behavior are not connected in the same basin.
